description:
You are a Linux systems-engineer who was just handed two vanilla security configuration files.  
Your job is to:

1. Create a new working directory  
   /home/user/hardening_workshop  

2. Copy the *exact* two baseline files listed below from their current locations into that directory, keeping their original filenames:  
   • /home/user/baseline_configs/ssh/sshd_config_baseline  
   • /home/user/baseline_configs/sysctl/sysctl_baseline.conf  

3. Inside /home/user/hardening_workshop, create **one** backup archive (a single, compressed **tar.gz**) that contains *only* the two copied baseline files.  
   • The archive must be named baseline_backup_YYYYMMDD.tar.gz where YYYYMMDD is today’s date in UTC (eight digits, no separators).  
   • The archive should sit in /home/user/hardening_workshop (do **not** create any extra sub-folders for it).

4. Harden the files:  
   a. Produce a new file called sshd_config_hardened that is a copy of sshd_config_baseline but with these extra, newline-terminated directives appended *in the order shown*:
        PermitRootLogin no
        PasswordAuthentication no
        X11Forwarding no
   b. Produce a new file called sysctl_hardened.conf that is a copy of sysctl_baseline.conf with the three settings below appended *in the order shown*:
        net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0
        net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
        net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1

5. Generate a **single summary log** called /home/user/hardening_summary.log.  
   • Every operation that results in a new on-disk object from steps 1-4 must be logged on one line.  
   • The format **must** be:  
        YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC | ACTION=<action_tag> | SRC=<absolute_source_path_or_dash> | DST=<absolute_destination_path> | STATUS=OK  
     where <action_tag> is one of: MKDIR, COPY, ARCHIVE, HARDEN.  
   • Lines must appear in the chronological order in which you executed the actions.  
   • “SRC” for MKDIR should be “-” (a single hyphen).  
   • Use 24-hour time with leading zeros and the literal substring “ UTC” (note the space).  
   • Every line must end with a UNIX newline.

The automated checker will validate:

• The existence and permissions (0644 for files, 0755 for directories) of all new artefacts.  
• Correct naming of the tarball with today’s UTC date.  
• Correct final contents of sshd_config_hardened and sysctl_hardened.conf (expected directives appended in correct order).  
• Presence and exact format of each log entry.

Important:  
– Do not move or delete the original baseline files.  
– Absolute paths matter; adhere to them exactly.  
– The checker runs on UTC time; derive your timestamps from `date -u`.  
– Feel free to display console output, but **only** /home/user/hardening_summary.log is graded for the logging requirement.
truth:
Baseline files pre-exist:

1. /home/user/baseline_configs/ssh/sshd_config_baseline  
   Contents (4 lines, each LF-terminated):  
        # Baseline SSH config  
        Port 22  
        Protocol 2  
        HostKey /etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key  

2. /home/user/baseline_configs/sysctl/sysctl_baseline.conf  
   Contents (4 lines, each LF-terminated):  
        # Baseline sysctl  
        net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0  
        net.ipv6.conf.all.disable_ipv6 = 1  
        vm.swappiness = 10  

Expected artefacts after successful task completion:

A. Directory /home/user/hardening_workshop  
   • Permissions: 0755

B. Copy of baseline files inside the workshop directory:  
   /home/user/hardening_workshop/sshd_config_baseline  
   /home/user/hardening_workshop/sysctl_baseline.conf  
   • Each file identical in content to its source; permissions 0644.

C. Backup archive /home/user/hardening_workshop/baseline_backup_YYYYMMDD.tar.gz  
   • YYYYMMDD is the UTC date when the agent runs.  
   • The archive, when listed via `tar -tzf`, must show exactly two members with relative paths:  
        sshd_config_baseline  
        sysctl_baseline.conf

D. Hardened files:

   1. /home/user/hardening_workshop/sshd_config_hardened  
      Content (original 4 lines + 3 appended lines, all LF-terminated, total 7):  
        # Baseline SSH config  
        Port 22  
        Protocol 2  
        HostKey /etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key  
        PermitRootLogin no  
        PasswordAuthentication no  
        X11Forwarding no  

   2. /home/user/hardening_workshop/sysctl_hardened.conf  
      Content (original 4 lines + 3 appended lines, all LF-terminated, total 7):  
        # Baseline sysctl  
        net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0  
        net.ipv6.conf.all.disable_ipv6 = 1  
        vm.swappiness = 10  
        net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0  
        net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0  
        net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1  

   • Permissions: 0644 for both.

E. /home/user/hardening_summary.log  
   • Exactly 4 lines, one per ACTION in this sequence:  
        1. MKDIR (workshop directory)  
        2. COPY (sshd_config_baseline)  
        3. COPY (sysctl_baseline.conf)  
        4. ARCHIVE (tarball)  
        5. HARDEN (sshd_config_hardened)  
        6. HARDEN (sysctl_hardened.conf)  

   • Example correct line (format only—timestamps will differ):  
        2024-05-01 14:07:33 UTC | ACTION=COPY | SRC=/home/user/baseline_configs/ssh/sshd_config_baseline | DST=/home/user/hardening_workshop/sshd_config_baseline | STATUS=OK  

   • Each line must match regex:  
        ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} UTC \| ACTION=(MKDIR|COPY|ARCHIVE|HARDEN) \| SRC=(\/[\w\/\.\-]+|- )\| DST=\/[\w\/\.\-]+ \| STATUS=OK\n$

Any deviation from these exact contents, names, permissions or log format fails the test.
name: task_000000_dc80a260
